text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
CYTOPATHOLOGY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Margaret Okonkwo
MRN: [REDACTED]
DATE OF BIRTH: [REDACTED]    AGE: 52    SEX: Female
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: [Head and Neck Surgery / Otolaryngology Attending]
CLINICAL SERVICE: Otolaryngology - Head and Neck Surgery
ACCESSION NUMBER: [CYT-XXXXXX]
DATE OF PROCEDURE: [See administrative record]
DATE OF RECEIPT: [See administrative record]
DATE OF REPORT: [See administrative record]
PATHOLOGIST: [Attending Cytopathologist, MD]
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN SUBMITTED:
Specimen A: Fine needle aspiration, left level IIA cervical lymph node, ultrasound-guided; direct smears (Papanicolaou-stained and Diff-Quik-stained), liquid-based preparation (ThinPrep), and cell block material.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CLINICAL HISTORY:
The patient is a 52-year-old never-smoking female of Nigerian-American descent who presents with a recently biopsied left base of tongue mass (prior pathology: squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive, HPV RNA ISH-positive) and a CT/PET-CT-demonstrated left level IIA cervical lymph node measuring 2.6-2.7 cm with central necrosis (SUVmax 11.8 on PET-CT), concerning for metastatic adenopathy. The procedure is performed for staging confirmation.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PROCEDURE DESCRIPTION:
Ultrasound-guided fine needle aspiration of the left level IIA cervical lymph node was performed by the cytopathology service using a 25-gauge needle with 3 passes. Rapid on-site evaluation (ROSE) was performed by the cytopathologist at the time of the procedure, with the preliminary assessment indicating adequate cellular material and suspicious for malignancy. Material was processed for direct smears (fixed and air-dried), liquid-based cytology (ThinPrep), and cell block preparation for ancillary immunohistochemical studies. The procedure was performed without complication under conscious sedation with ultrasound guidance. No immediate adverse events were noted.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received for rapid on-site evaluation and permanent processing:
- Pass 1: Four direct smears prepared; 2 fixed in 95% ethanol (Papanicolaou stain), 2 air-dried (Diff-Quik stain). Residual needle rinse submitted in CytoLyt fixative for ThinPrep preparation.
- Pass 2: Four direct smears prepared; 2 fixed and 2 air-dried. Residual material submitted in CytoLyt fixative.
- Pass 3: Material submitted entirely into CytoLyt fixative for cell block preparation. The aspirate was frankly cellular, with turbid tan-red fluid noted in the aspirate consistent with a necrotic nodal background.
Cell block: Prepared from combined passes. Following centrifugation, the pellet was fixed in 10% neutral buffered formalin, embedded in paraffin, and sectioned at 5-micron levels. Sections are tan-pink and grossly unremarkable. Multiple levels obtained for H&E staining and immunohistochemical studies.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSIS:

Specimen A -- LEFT LEVEL IIA CERVICAL LYMPH NODE, ULTRASOUND-GUIDED FINE NEEDLE ASPIRATION:

METASTATIC SQUAMOUS CELL CARCINOMA
- Consistent with oropharyngeal primary (see note)
- p16 (CDKN2A) immunostain: STRONGLY AND DIFFUSELY POSITIVE (see ancillary studies)
- Background necrotic debris present
- No morphologic evidence of keratinizing squamous cell carcinoma

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
The smears are cellular and consist of cohesive and single atypical epithelial cells set against a background of abundant necrotic debris, lymphocyte-rich inflammatory infiltrate, and occasional macrophages consistent with necrotic nodal tissue. The malignant cells are polygonal to spindled, with moderate to abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm, irregular nuclear membranes, coarse chromatin, and prominent nucleoli. Intercellular bridges are focally appreciated on Diff-Quik-stained air-dried smears. Keratinization is not identified; no keratin pearls are seen. The cytomorphology is that of a non-keratinizing squamous cell carcinoma.

On the ThinPrep preparation, similar malignant epithelial cells are identified in both tight syncytial clusters and as scattered single cells. Atypical mitotic figures are present. No glandular or mucinous differentiation is identified.

The cell block sections show islands and nests of malignant squamous epithelium within a fibrotic and necrotic lymph node background. Lymphoid elements are effaced and largely replaced by tumor deposits. The histomorphology is consistent with metastatic non-keratinizing squamous cell carcinoma involving a cervical lymph node, with central nodal necrosis.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES:

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on cell block sections):

1. p16 (CINtec, clone E6H4, prediluted; Roche): STRONGLY AND DIFFUSELY POSITIVE
   - Staining pattern: Strong, diffuse nuclear and cytoplasmic positivity in >90% of malignant cells
   - Interpretation: Block-positive pattern (meets accepted threshold of >70% cells with strong diffuse nuclear and cytoplasmic staining)
   - This staining pattern is consistent with transcriptionally-active high-risk HPV infection and is consistent with HPV-associated oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma origin
   - Appropriate positive and negative controls performed and verified

2. p40 (clone BC28, prediluted): POSITIVE in malignant cells, confirming squamous lineage

3. CK5/6 (clone D5/16 B4, prediluted): POSITIVE in malignant cells, confirming squamous differentiation

4. TTF-1 (clone 8G7G3/1, prediluted): NEGATIVE in malignant cells (argues against pulmonary primary)

5. Napsin A: NEGATIVE

6. CK7: Focally weakly positive in malignant cells (non-specific pattern)

HIGH-RISK HPV RNA IN SITU HYBRIDIZATION (RNAscope, Advanced Cell Diagnostics):
- RESULT: PENDING at time of preliminary report issuance; will be appended upon completion
- Note: Given the unequivocal block-positive p16 immunostaining and clinical context of a known HPV-positive oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma primary, results are expected to confirm HPV RNA expression. Preliminary diagnosis issued per current College of American Pathologists (CAP) / ASCO guidelines allowing p16 IHC as surrogate for HPV status in cervical lymph node metastases from known oropharyngeal primary.

Note: EBV-encoded RNA (EBER) ISH was not performed at this time. Given the squamous morphology, p40/CK5-6 positivity, and p16 block-positive staining in the clinical setting of a known p16-positive oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma, EBV-associated nasopharyngeal carcinoma is not favored; EBER ISH may be added at the discretion of the clinical team or upon amended request.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
NOTE / INTERPRETATION:
The cytomorphology of the aspirate from the left level IIA cervical lymph node is that of metastatic non-keratinizing squamous cell carcinoma. In the clinical context of a confirmed, recently biopsied left base of tongue squamous cell carcinoma (p16-positive, HPV RNA ISH-positive; prior accession [see institutional cross-reference]), this nodal metastasis is most consistent with involvement from the known oropharyngeal primary.

The strongly diffuse block-positive p16 immunostaining pattern (>90% cells, nuclear and cytoplasmic) on the cell block is consistent with transcriptionally-active high-risk HPV-associated carcinoma and correlates directly with p16 positivity documented at the primary site. This finding, in conjunction with the non-keratinizing squamous morphology, is consistent with HPV-driven oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma metastatic to a cervical lymph node.

The presence of extensive background necrosis in the aspirate is consistent with the central necrosis previously identified on CT and PET-CT imaging of this node.

These findings support regional (N1) nodal metastasis from the known primary HPV-positive oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma. Correlation with clinical and imaging staging information is recommended.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSTIC SUMMARY:
- Specimen adequacy: ADEQUATE
- Cellularity: Moderately cellular
- Final diagnosis: Metastatic squamous cell carcinoma, p16 strongly positive, consistent with oropharyngeal primary origin
- Ancillary study status: p16 IHC complete (positive); HPV RNA ISH pending (addendum to follow)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SIGNED:
[Attending Cytopathologist, MD]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
Electronic signature applied upon final review

[Resident/Fellow, MD - transcription]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ADDENDUM (to be issued upon completion of pending studies):
High-risk HPV RNA ISH result will be appended to this report upon finalization.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Distribution: Ordering Physician, Electronic Medical Record, Head and Neck Tumor Board File
